news.title: Morgan Stanley retains Overweight rating for Apollo Hospitals, target price at 9,209.

news.title:
Apollo Hospitals faces a short-term risk due to West Asia exposure worth around ₹50 Cr.
Apollo Hospitals shared this update during an interaction with CNBC TV18.
news.summary:

news.title:
Apollo Hospitals earns 6% revenue from medical tourism, mainly Bangladesh.
Expansion planned to CIS, Indonesia; West Asia risk exposure of ₹50 crore.

news.title:
Apollo Hospitals saw a block trade of 166,758 shares at Rs. 7591.50.
The trade totaled Rs. 126.59 crores on NSE.

news.title:
Apollo Hospitals shared the transcript of its Q3 FY26 results presentation.
The transcript is available on the company's website for public access.
news.summary:

news.title:
Apollo Hospitals has released the audio recording of its analyst call on financial results for the three and nine months ending Dec 31, 2025.
The recording is available on the company's website, adhering to SEBI regulations, for stakeholder access.
news.summary:

news.title:
Apollo Hospitals sees 8% volume growth due to rising insurance tie-ups.
Q2 insurance agreements positively impacted Q3 business volumes.
news.summary:
Apollo Hospitals sees 8% volume growth due to rising insurance tie-ups.

news.title: Apollo Hospitals surpasses Q3 estimates as specialty care boosts growth.
news.summary: Apollo Hospitals surpasses Q3 estimates as specialty care boosts growth.

news.title:
Apollo Hospitals' consolidated revenue grew 14% YoY to ₹64,774M; EBITDA margin at 24.8%.
Healthcare services revenue rose 14% YoY with 67% occupancy; digital health GMV up 28%.
news.summary:
Apollo Hospitals' consolidated revenue grew 14% YoY to ₹64,774M; EBITDA margin at 24.8%.
Healthcare services revenue rose 14% YoY with 67% occupancy; digital health GMV up 28%.

news.title:
Apollo Hospitals' subsidiary acquired 100% stake in Belenus Champion Hospitals Pvt Ltd.
This acquisition aims to expand and upgrade hospital operations in Bangalore.
news.summary:
Apollo Hospitals' subsidiary acquired 100% stake in Belenus Champion Hospitals Pvt Ltd.
This acquisition aims to expand and upgrade hospital operations in Bangalore.

news.title:
Apollo Hospitals announced Rs.10/share interim dividend for FY2025-26.
Q3 consolidated revenue stood at Rs.64,774 mn, EBITDA at Rs.20,442 mn.
news.summary:
Apollo Hospitals announced Rs.10/share interim dividend for FY2025-26.

news.title:
Major Q3 results this week include Hindalco, ONGC, Britannia, M&M, and Ashok Leyland.
Other key companies reporting include Titan, IRCTC, Eicher Motors, Pfizer, and Apollo Hospitals.

news.title:
Apollo Hospitals will host a Q3 FY26 earnings call on Feb 11, 2026 at 2 PM IST.
The call will discuss Dec 2025 results, with details on www.apollohospitals.com.
news.summary:

news.title:
CRISIL reaffirmed Apollo Hospitals' AA rating, upgrading outlook to Positive citing strong market position and financials.
Rating supported by a diversified business mix, solid liquidity, and moderate capex amid regulatory risks and digital platform losses.
news.summary:
CRISIL reaffirmed Apollo Hospitals' AA rating, upgrading outlook to Positive citing strong market position and financials.
Rating supported by a diversified business mix, solid liquidity, and moderate capex amid regulatory risks and digital platform losses.

news.title: Citi raises Apollo Hospitals' target price to INR 9,600, citing growth confidence.

news.title:
Apollo Hospitals gets approval to acquire 30.6% in Apollo Health & Lifestyle.
This move strengthens Apollo Hospitals' position in the healthcare sector.
news.summary:
Apollo Hospitals gets approval to acquire 30.6% in Apollo Health & Lifestyle.

news.title:
Pharma firms expect 8-11% revenue growth in Q3FY26 driven by strong domestic sales.
Generic Revlimid sales decline in the US; PAT growth forecasted at 2-4% for the quarter.
news.summary:

news.title:
Apollo Hospitals Board to meet on Feb 10, 2026, to approve Q3 financial results.
Trading window remains closed till Feb 12, 2026, as per insider trading regulations.
news.summary:

news.title:
India's worsening air quality boosts respiratory drug sales, with the market valued at Rs 18,913 crore and growing at 8% CAGR.
Anti-asthma and COPD products dominate with 61% market share; Apollo Hospitals highlights pollution as a key trigger for respiratory issues.
news.summary:
India's worsening air quality boosts respiratory drug sales, with the market valued at Rs 18,913 crore and growing at 8% CAGR.
Anti-asthma and COPD products dominate with 61% market share; Apollo Hospitals highlights pollution as a key trigger for respiratory issues.
